channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hamilton Leads Ferrari One-Two in Monaco Grand Prix Second Practice Session
item.description: Lewis Hamilton delivered an impressive performance during the second practice session (FP2) for the Monaco Grand Prix, setting the fastest lap time of 1:13.026 for Ferrari. The seven-time Formula One ...

item.title: Prince Harry expected to miss Peter Phillips's wedding amid longstanding family tensions
item.description: Prince Harry is reportedly expected to be absent from the wedding of his cousin Peter Phillips and Harriet Sperling in the Cotswolds. According to the article, the decision is linked not only to Harry...

item.title: US and Iran Remain Divided Over Asset Release and Nuclear Commitments as Negotiations Continue
item.description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran over a potential agreement aimed at resolving tensions surrounding Iran’s nuclear program remain active but face significant obstacles. According to rep...

item.title: Knicks Defeat Spurs 105-104 in Game 2 to Take 2-0 Lead in NBA Finals
item.description: The New York Knicks moved within two victories of an NBA championship after defeating the San Antonio Spurs 105-104 in Game 2 of the 2026 NBA Finals. The game was decided in the closing seconds when J...

item.title: Startup founders recount negative venture capital experiences and industry behavior concerns
item.description: A widely discussed thread on X has brought together startup founders sharing negative experiences with venture capitalists, revealing a range of unflattering anecdotes about fundraising interactions. ...

item.title: Super Falcons defeat Senegal 2-1 in WAFCON preparation friendly in Ikenne-Remo
item.description: Nigeria’s Super Falcons started their preparations for the upcoming Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) on a positive note as they secured a 2-1 victory over Senegal’s Teranga Lionesses in a friend...

item.title: AJK government bans JAAC under Anti-Terrorism Act and adds it to First Schedule amid planned protests
item.description: The Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) government has formally declared the Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC) as a proscribed organisation under the Anti-Terrorism Act, 2014. According to a ...

item.title: Actor Anthony Head, known for Buffy the Vampire Slayer, Ted Lasso and Merlin, dies aged 72
item.description: British actor Anthony Head has died at the age of 72 following complications from pneumonia, according to a statement released by his daughters, Emily and Daisy Head. They said he passed away peaceful...

item.title: NTSB report details Newark landing incident involving United Airlines flight and light pole strike
item.description: A preliminary report from the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) has provided new details about an incident involving a United Airlines passenger flight that struck a light pole while landing...

item.title: Court adjourns judgment on suit seeking deregistration of ADC and other political parties
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has adjourned indefinitely its proceedings in a case seeking the deregistration of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) and several other political parties in Nigeria....

item.title: Petrol Price Reduced by Rs4 per Litre While Diesel Remains at Rs380.78 for Another Week
item.description: The federal government has announced a reduction of Rs4 per litre in the price of petrol, while keeping the price of high-speed diesel (HSD) unchanged for the week ending June 12, 2026. Following the ...

item.title: Escalation in Gulf tensions as US intercepts Iranian missiles and drones amid ongoing Iran–US conflict
item.description: Tensions between the United States and Iran have escalated sharply following a series of military exchanges in the Gulf region. According to US Central Command, American forces intercepted six Iranian...

item.title: Report: ICE Plans Nationwide Expansion of Facial Recognition App for Local Police Despite Accuracy Concerns
item.description: Internal government documents reportedly indicate that U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) is preparing to distribute a facial recognition application known as the ICE Task Force Module to ...

item.title: Critics Question Trump's Mental Sharpness After Wisconsin Remarks Referencing the Washington Monument
item.description: A video clip from President Donald Trump's June 5, 2026, agriculture roundtable in Chippewa Falls, Wisconsin, generated significant online discussion after he appeared to struggle momentarily to recal...

item.title: Susie Wiles denies resignation rumors and reaffirms commitment to White House role
item.description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles publicly dismissed reports suggesting she may soon leave the Trump administration, responding directly on X (formerly Twitter) after media speculation circulated...

item.title: King Kaka opens up on shock divorce and life as a single father while considering new relationship
item.description: Veteran Kenyan musician King Kaka has spoken openly about the end of his 13-year marriage to fellow public figure Nana Owiti, saying the separation came as a shock to him, just as it did to many of th...

item.title: US Military Conducts Strikes on Iranian Radar Facilities Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: According to the available article information, the United States carried out strikes targeting radar installations in Iran. The report, published by Agence France-Presse and carried by ABS-CBN News o...

item.title: Nish Kumar discusses comedy, free speech, and responsibility towards marginalised groups
item.description: In an in-depth interview ahead of his new stand-up tour, British comedian Nish Kumar reflects on his career, his approach to comedy, and the increasingly polarised debates surrounding free speech and ...

item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and warnings amid escalating tensions near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iran have escalated military tensions in the Gulf region following a series of drone incidents and retaliatory strikes near the strategic Strait of Hormuz. According to US Centra...

item.title: John Carney's 'Power Ballad' with Paul Rudd and Nick Jonas Celebrates Authentic Music Creation in Film
item.description: John Carney, the acclaimed writer-director known for films like 'Once' and 'Sing Street,' returns with 'Power Ballad,' a music-focused comedy-drama starring Paul Rudd as an Ireland-based wedding singe...
